summary: Joel catalogs twelve distinct deceptions on a single piece of Earthlink junk mail designed to mimic a FedEx express delivery. He systematically dissects every element—from fake tracking numbers and forged handwriting fonts to a pointless piece of clear plastic—showing how direct mailers will use any trick to get recipients to open their mail. He contrasts these tactics with Earthlink's stated corporate value of 'complete honesty and integrity in everything we do.' The post uses humor and detailed forensic analysis to highlight the fundamental dishonesty baked into direct mail marketing.
key_points: The Earthlink mailer uses at least twelve distinct deceptions to disguise junk mail as an urgent express delivery, including fake tracking numbers, forged signatures, and misleading weight limits
Direct mail's core problem—99% gets thrown away unopened—drives marketers to increasingly dishonest tactics to trick recipients into opening envelopes
The biological concept of Batesian mimicry perfectly describes how junk mail imitates legitimate express mail packaging to exploit recipients' trust
Corporate mission statements about honesty and integrity ring hollow when the company's own marketing department employs systematic deception
Each individual lie seems small, but the cumulative effect reveals a marketing culture where deception is the default rather than the exception
money_quotes: The marketing slimeballs at Earthlink must have drooled at the thought of old Marge in a nursing home, practically falling over with excitement that she got an urgent express letter.
Sorry, folks, for $0.23 you don't get tracking.
People won't open it unless it's from someone they know, and everyone knows Sky Dayton!

The clear plastic serves no purpose whatsoever except to imitate FedEx envelopes which have the same plastic to hold the mailing manifest, much like those little butterflies which have fierce markings in attempt to convince butterfly-eaters that they themselves are fierce.
The biggest problem with direct mail (what we normal people call 'Junk Mail') is that 99% of it (literally) gets thrown away unopened, Batesian mimicry or not.
To combat this, direct mailers will do anything to get you to open their junk, no matter how dishonest.
We require complete honesty and integrity in everything we do.
key_insight: The desperation of direct mail marketing—where 99% of pieces are discarded unopened—creates a race to the bottom in honesty, producing elaborate deceptions that directly contradict companies' stated values.
